outline_json.title: The Arbiter's Arrival

scenes.description: Maret enters the Scribe's Hall, nods to the senior scribe, and takes her seat in the alcove. She checks the day's intake ledger for any new files mentioning anomalies, a habit born of paranoia.
Maret spots a sealed dispatch from the capital, marked 'Arbiter Cassel — Sector 7.' Her throat tightens as she reads it; the Arbiter is coming about a statistical anomaly—her anomaly.
Maret forces herself to breathe. She thinks of the iron bar she bent yesterday, now hidden under a loose floorboard at home. She must not react when questioned.
Journeyman Theo approaches Maret's desk, leaning casually. He asks if she's heard about the Arbiter's visit, noting her tension. She deflects with a comment about paperwork, but he doesn't press.

The main door opens. Arbiter Cassel enters, his dark robes and silver badge marking his authority. He announces his purpose to the room—an investigation into a statistical anomaly in the sector—and his gaze settles on Maret.

Cassel requests Maret's personnel file and a private workspace. Maret leads him to a side room, her voice steady as she offers to fetch the records. He notes her professionalism but watches her hands.
Maret retrieves her file from the archive, resisting the urge to alter it. She hands it to Cassel, who opens it immediately and scans the stat page.

Cassel reads aloud: Strength 3. He then places a witness statement on the desk—a blacksmith who claims Maret bent an iron bar with her bare hands. 'Explain this,' he says. Maret denies it, calling the witness mistaken.

Maret's heart pounds, but she keeps her face neutral. She knows one wrong word will expose her. She deflects by questioning the witness's reliability and offering to submit to a simple strength test—which she plans to fail.
Cassel accepts the proposed test but warns that the System does not err. He closes the file and informs her he will observe her for the next three days. He leaves the room, but his presence lingers.
Theo appears at the door. He says quietly that he knows she's hiding something, but that he'll back her story if she needs it. His offer is sincere; she thanks him but says nothing.

Alone at her desk, Maret stares at the row of ledgers. She knows Cassel will not stop until he proves the System right—or wrong. She begins planning her next move, but every path leads toward exposure.

outline_json.purpose: Establish Maret's precarious routine of hiding her true strength behind false stats, and shatter it when Arbiter Cassel arrives to investigate the anomaly—forcing her to confront the immediate threat of exposure while she tries to deflect his scrutiny through careful paperwork.

Arbiter Cassel firmly believes the System does not make mistakes

outline_json.title: The System's Trap

scenes.description: Maret tries to access the System records on her stats but discovers Cassel has already flagged and locked the relevant data, blocking any alteration attempt.
Arbiter Cassel enters the scribe hall and confronts Maret about the anomaly, asking pointed questions about her stat history and recent activities.
Maret deflects using technical jargon and references to standard procedures, but Cassel remains unconvinced and notes every evasion.
Maret leaves the hall and meets Theo at the Hearthfire Inn, where she partially admits the investigation is worse than she thought.
Theo offers to use his adventurer contacts to dig into Cassel's background, but Maret is reluctant to involve him further.
Cassel summons Maret to his office and presents the evidence of her failed record tampering, showing her own signature in the access log.
Cassel produces a sealed file from the System's central archives, marked with a date eight years ago, and places it on the desk.
Maret opens the file and reads the System's own notation: her stat discrepancy was noted at her Binding and deliberately left uncorrected without explanation.
Cassel explains that the System's deliberate omission means this is no mere error but a fundamental anomaly, and only a Revelation Rite can resolve it.
Cassel threatens to petition for the revocation of Journeyman Theo's class if Maret does not voluntarily submit to the Revelation Rite within twenty-four hours.
Maret's mind races as she realizes the choice: reveal her true nature or let Theo lose everything he has.
Maret tries to negotiate a delay or alternative, but Cassel is immovable, citing System protocols and his Arbiter authority.
Cassel grants her until dawn to decide, then dismisses her with the warning that Theo's fate is now in her hands.
Maret walks through the city streets alone, replaying Cassel's words and the sealed file, feeling the weight of eight years of deception.
Theo finds Maret at the Hearthfire Inn and offers reassurance, but she cannot tell him the full truth; she sends him away for his own safety.
Alone, Maret makes her decision: she will submit to the Revelation Rite to protect Theo, whatever the cost to herself.
The next morning, Maret walks toward the Arbiter's Office, her steps steady but her heart pounding, ready to face the truth.

outline_json.purpose: Maret's attempts to falsify records and appear normal fail as Cassel tightens his investigation, culminating in a sealed file revealing the System deliberately left her stats uncorrected for eight years—then Cassel threatens to revoke Theo's class unless she submits to the Revelation Rite, forcing her to choose between her secret and her friend.
outline_json.setting: The Scribe's Hall, The Hearthfire Inn, The Arbiter's Office

outline_json.title: The True Number

scenes.description: Maret stands at the center of the Guild Council Chamber, facing the System crystal on its pedestal. Arbiter Cassel and Journeyman Theo watch from opposite sides of the circular table, the tension palpable as the Revelation Rite begins.

Cassel formally recites the terms of the Revelation Rite, his voice echoing in the domed hall. He reads from a System-issued scroll, detailing that Maret's class and true stats will be revealed and recorded, and that she must accept any consequences.

Maret nods once, steps forward, and presses her palm flat against the cool surface of the crystal. The chamber lights dim as the System begins scanning her, blue light pulsing through her veins from the point of contact.

Instead of displaying her false stats, the crystal projects a cascade of floating numbers—then freezes on a single line: 'Class: Anomaly – Authority: Anomaly Hunter.' The air hums with the System's validation, a sound Maret has never heard before.

Cassel's composure cracks—his eyes widen, and he takes a half-step back before catching himself. He rereads the floating text as if it might change, but the System holds steady, a soft chime confirming the record is permanent.
Theo releases a breath he'd been holding, a grin spreading across his face. He sees Maret's true class and understands the weight of her secret at last, his acceptance clear in his posture.

A new message scrolls across the crystal: 'Anomaly validated. Existence consistent with System design. Deploy to investigate further aberrations.' The words solidify Maret's new role, transforming her from suspect to agent of the System itself.

Cassel recovers his analytical demeanor and steps forward, extending his hand to Maret. He proposes an alliance: he will officially close the case and recommend her appointment as an anomaly hunter, if she agrees to work with him to investigate other System discrepancies.

Maret hesitates, then takes his hand. She accepts the terms: she will report to Cassel, but will retain autonomy in the field. The handshake seals the uneasy compact, witnessed by Theo and the silent Guild Council.

The crystal pulses once more and withdraws the display. Maret's hand comes away warm, the scent of ozone lingering. She now knows her true class is Anomaly—neither human standard nor deviation, but a role the System itself created.

Cassel signals to a scribe outside, who enters with a new contract. The document outlines Maret's duties as an anomaly hunter, including a stipend and access to restricted archives. He places it on the table for her to sign.

Maret reviews the contract silently, noting the clauses that grant her unusual freedom—her status is unique, and the System seems to have written the terms itself. She signs with her scribe's quill, the ink glowing briefly as the System records her mark.

After the signing, Cassel outlines the first mission: a village two days north reports creatures acting with unnatural intelligence. He expects Maret to leave at dawn. Theo volunteers to accompany her, and Cassel approves without objection.

Maret looks at the crystal one last time, her reflection clear in its surface. She feels the weight of her new responsibility—and the first flicker of freedom in eight years. She turns and walks out of the chamber, Theo falling into step beside her, the doors closing behind them.

outline_json.purpose: In the Revelation Rite, Maret chooses to reveal her true class—Anomaly—and the System validates her existence, transforming her from fugitive to authorized anomaly hunter; she and Cassel forge an uneasy alliance, leaving her with hard-won freedom but the weight of a new responsibility.

customs_json: All Arbiters wear a silver badge shaped like a balanced scale.
They perform daily System calibration rituals to ensure their own stats are accurate.
They never display their own stats publicly—it is considered 'undue influence'.
When investigating, they keep a personal log that is System-encrypted.
cultures.description: A secretive order of investigators dedicated to preserving the integrity of the System. They are chosen from high-level individuals and are given the authority to override local laws. They operate from the capital and are seen as both respected and feared.
cultures.name: The Divine Concord (Arbiters)
cultures.speech_influences: Speak in deliberate, measured tones. Use precise System terminology. Often use formal titles: 'Arbiter Cassel' not 'Cassel'. Avoid small talk, stay on topic. Common phrase: 'The System does not err.'

system_views_json.sys_framework: The System is the ultimate authority. Their job is to protect it from corruption and anomaly. They believe anomalies are threats that must be neutralized.

taboos_json: Disobeying a direct order from the High Arbiter

customs_json: Each scribe has a personal seal used to authenticate documents.
Daily check-in with the Guild Master to report any data irregularities.
Training includes memorizing System protocols and legal codes.
They are expected to remain neutral in guild disputes, even if their personal leanings differ.
cultures.description: A guild of administrators, scholars, and record-keepers. They manage System records, local census data, and legal documentation. They are non-combatants by class but can be influential. They value knowledge and accuracy.
cultures.name: The Scribe's Guild
cultures.speech_influences: Precise, literate, and often verbose. They use formal grammar and avoid slang. Common phrases: 'Per the System record', 'Let the ledger speak'. They tend to avoid direct confrontation and speak in passive voice.

system_views_json.sys_framework: The System is a tool to be cataloged and managed. They respect its authority but are aware of its complexities and occasional inconsistencies (though they would never openly say so).

taboos_json: Using one's position for personal gain

customs_json: Greetings often include a mention of class: 'Greetings, Scribe Maret.'
Before dangerous quests, adventurers touch their class sigil (a tattoo) for luck.
Weekly guild meetings where members discuss quests and progress.
Annual Festival of Binding, celebrating the System's gift with parades and contests.
cultures.description: Inhabitants of Veridell, a diverse guild-based society. They are pragmatic, self-reliant, and respectful of the System. Adventurers are common; scribes are essential administrators.
cultures.name: Veridellians
cultures.speech_influences: Formal in guild settings, but casual among friends. Common phrases: 'By the System', 'May your stats rise', 'That's an anomaly' (used for any odd event). Avoid discussing suspected data fraud openly.

system_views_json.sys_framework: Revered as the foundation of society. The System's rules are seen as natural law. Anomalies are feared but believed to be rare and dangerous.

taboos_json: Deserting your guild in a crisis

seed_json.premise: In a world governed by the System — a divine framework that assigns every person a class, tracks their statistics, and rewards growth with tangible power — a young scribe's stats have been lying since she received them at fourteen. Her Strength reads 3. She has bent iron bars with her hands. The System has never corrected the discrepancy. Now a System Arbiter has arrived in her city to investigate an anomaly — her anomaly.

characters.name: Maret
characters.description: Scribe, early 20s. Has lived with false stats for eight years by hiding what she can do. Uses her official Scribe class as cover — the administrative access it grants lets her avoid most physical situations. Smart, secretive, deeply uncertain whether the System is protecting her or preparing something. Has never told anyone her real numbers.

characters.name: Journeyman Theo
characters.description: A young adventurer in Maret's city. His stats are genuinely mediocre — he knows exactly what he is and is at peace with it. Befriended Maret two years ago because she's smarter than anyone in his party. Has noticed that she avoids combat situations in ways that don't match a Scribe's normal reasons. Doesn't push. Asks good questions.

characters.name: Arbiter Cassel
characters.description: System Arbiter, mid-40s. Investigates discrepancies between System records and observed reality. Genuinely believes in the System's justice — the Framework doesn't make mistakes, so anomalies must be corrected. His case file has Maret flagged as Threat Level: Unknown. He's not wrong to be cautious. He also doesn't know the System flagged her anomaly eight years ago and chose not to correct it.

constraints_json: The System cannot create something from nothing; it only catalogs and rewards effort.
It cannot take away a class without a high-level ritual (forbidden except in extreme cases).
It does not reveal the future or hidden information—only what is directly observed or recorded.
Anomalies are outside its understanding; it can only flag them for Arbiter intervention.
world_systems.description: A divine, omnipresent framework that assigns classes, tracks stats, grants skills, and governs leveling. It is believed to be infallible and just. The System manifests as translucent blue interface screens, audible chimes, and floating numbers for stats and levels. It enforces the 'laws of reality' in this world.
manifestations_json: People see stat numbers floating above others' heads (if they choose to display them).
Skill descriptions appear in the mind when focusing on a spell or technique.
System Messages: 'You have gained experience' or 'New skill unlocked' appear as blue text with a chime.
world_systems.name: The System
rules_json: Every person receives a class at age 14 during a Binding Ceremony, performed by a priest of the Divine Concord.
Stats are measured from 1–100 (human baseline). Leveling increases stats and may unlock new abilities or class evolutions.
Certain actions (like killing monsters) grant experience. Quests can give bonus XP or items.
The System cannot be deceived by mortals—any attempt to falsify stats is always detected, except in cases of true anomaly.
Anomalies are rare and are presumed to be errors in reality; they must be corrected by Arbiters.
